# Prepare normalization and search
import re, unicodedata
def normalize_ar(text):
    if text is None:
        return ""
    # strip tashkeel and diacritics
    # patterns for Arabic diacritics including superscript alef \u0670
    text = re.sub(r'[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]', '', text)
    # tatweel
    text = text.replace('\u0640','')
    # normalize hamza forms
    text = text.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    text = text.replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي').replace('ء','ء')
    # normalize alif maqsura to ya
    text = text.replace('ى','ي')
    # normalize taa marbuta to heh? but better to convert to ه
    # Usually convert 'ة' to 'ه' or 'ت'? I'll convert to 'ه'
    text = text.replace('ة','ه')
    # Remove punctuation and non-letters/digits
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\u0600-\u06FF\s]',' ', text)  # keep Arabic letters and underscores and spaces
    # collapse whitespace
    text = re.sub(r'\s+',' ', text).strip()
    # lowercase not relevant
    return text

full_span = "وَإِن تُبَذُّوا شَيْئًا لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ يَرْزُقْكُمْ وَيُزَيِّدْكُم مِنْ أَمْرِهِ وَإِن تُعْرِضُوا فَإِنَّهُ غَنِيٌ حَمِيدٌ"

# Normalize all ayah_text entries and search for substring matches
def prepare_db_norm(db):
    res = []
    for entry in db:
        txt = entry.get('ayah_text') if 'ayah_text' in entry else (entry.get('hadithTxt') or entry.get('Matn') or "")
        res.append({'entry':entry, 'text':txt, 'norm':normalize_ar(txt)})
    return res

# search for direct normalized substring

norm_full = normalize_ar(full_span)
for item in qnorm:
    if norm_full in item['norm']:
        q_matches.append(item)
len(q_matches)

# similarity helpers
def token_f1(a,b):
    # tokens split by space
    ta = a.split()
    tb = b.split()
    if not ta or not tb:
        return 0.0
    set_a = ta
    set_b = tb
    # count common tokens with multiplicity? Use multiset
    from collections import Counter
    ca = Counter(set_a)
    cb = Counter(set_b)
    common = sum((ca & cb).values())
    prec = common / len(set_b)
    rec = common / len(set_a)
    if prec+rec == 0:
        return 0.0
    return 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)

def longest_common_substring_len(a,b):
    # return length of LCS (contiguous) using dynamic programming
    # a and b are strings
    la = len(a); lb = len(b)
    if la==0 or lb==0:
        return 0
    # optimize by using smaller loops
    dp = [0]*(lb+1)
    maxl = 0
    for i in range(la):
        prev = 0
        for j in range(lb):
            temp = dp[j+1]
            if a[i]==b[j]:
                dp[j+1] = prev+1
                if dp[j+1] > maxl:
                    maxl = dp[j+1]
            else:
                dp[j+1] = 0
            prev = temp
    return maxl

def similarity(a,b):
    # normalize already assumed
    tf1 = token_f1(a,b)
    lcs = longest_common_substring_len(a,b)
    avg_len = (len(a)+len(b))/2 if (len(a)+len(b))>0 else 1
    lcs_ratio = lcs/avg_len
    # composite
    return 0.6*tf1 + 0.4*lcs_ratio, tf1, lcs_ratio

# compute top matches
scores = []
for item in qnorm:
    sim,tf1,lcs_r = similarity(norm_full, item['norm'])
    scores.append((sim, tf1, lcs_r, item))

# search for words with 'تبذ' or 'تبذر' 'نبذ' 'تبذر' etc in q and h
def find_substring_in_db(dbnorm, substr):
    ns = normalize_ar(substr)
    return [item for item in dbnorm if ns in item['norm']]

cands = find_substring_in_db(qnorm, "تبذ") + find_substring_in_db(qnorm, "تبذر") + find_substring_in_db(qnorm, "تبد") + find_substring_in_db(qnorm, "تبر")
